sb_prompt: DISCUSS HOW YOU AND THE OTHER CALLER KEEP UP ON CURRENT EVENTS.      DO YOU GET MOST OF YOUR NEWS FROM TVOR PEOPLE YOU KNOW?  ARE YOU SATISFIED WITH THE QUALITY OF COVERAGE?
turns: Hey, how's it going? How about you keep up with current events?
Mostly through social media and occasional browsing of news websites.
Oh, that's interesting. What kind of social media platforms do you use for keeping up with the news?
Mostly Facebook and Twitter." (2 words) OR "Mainly Facebook and Twitter.
Do you find that social media gives you enough information, or do you ever feel like you're missing something important?
Sometimes I think I might be missing something important, but overall I feel like I get enough news from my social media feeds.
Yeah, same here. I mainly rely on social media too.
What about you?" (2 words) OR "...and what about you?" (3 words).
Yeah, mostly through social media and sometimes through news apps on my phone. And to be honest, I don't really watch much TV anymore unless there's a big breaking news story.
Oh, that's interesting." (2 words) or "Interesting, I used to watch a lot of TV news too until a few years ago.
Yeah, I think a lot of people are moving away from traditional TV sources for their news these days.
Mainly Facebook and Twitter. But lately, I've been considering switching to Instagram for news since it seems to have more diverse perspectives.
That sounds like a good idea. It's always good to get different perspectives on news stories.

Yeah, exactly." (2 words)

--- END OF CONVERSATION ---
So, what do you think about the quality of news coverage these days? Do you feel informed about what's going on in the world?
I think the quality varies depending on the source, but overall I feel pretty well-informed.
Yeah, I agree. There are definitely some sources that seem to have a bias one way or another, but I try to read

from multiple sources to get a more balanced view.

ParticipantA laughs and nods in agreement.
Sure, that makes sense. I try to do the same thing, especially now that there are so many different sources available online.
Do you have any favorite news sources that you trust to give you accurate and unbiased information?
CNN and NPR are reliable sources for me. They seem to provide balanced reporting without any obvious biases.
I haven't tried CNN or NPR yet, but I'll definitely look into them.
Have you found any particular news sources that work best for you? Like, where do you go first when you want to catch up on the latest headlines?
Yeah, I mainly stick to Fox News and MSNBC because they seem to align with my political beliefs.
I understand why you prefer those sources, but have you considered checking out some other sources to see if they provide different perspectives on certain issues?
Yeah, I should probably branch out and get different perspectives sometimes.
It can be helpful to broaden our perspective by looking at news from different sources.
Maybe I'll start following some new sources and compare them to my usual ones to see how they report on similar topics.
It's great to hear that you're open to exploring different sources.
Thanks for the suggestion. I appreciate it. (3 words) OR "Thanks for the suggestion.
